title: Running LLMs locally with LM Studio
brief: What LM Studio is and why local LLMs; download + load a model (MLX on Apple Silicon); turn on the OpenAI-compatible server; call /v1/chat/completions; when local beats cloud (privacy, cost, offline) and when it doesn't.

title: Designing idempotent APIs
brief: Why retries and network flakiness make idempotency essential; idempotency keys; dedup windows; safe vs unsafe methods; making POST safe; how payment and ticket-creation flows benefit.

title: Giving a local LLM tools with MCP
brief: What the Model Context Protocol is; how an MCP host (LM Studio) connects to MCP servers; adding a server in mcp.json; tool-call approval; example: letting a local model query your own data; why MCP is the unlock for useful local agents.

title: Building XPRIZO: a multi-currency wallet in Flutter
brief: Built from scratch as lead of the India team with the client CTO; Flutter app for Android & iOS plus an Angular back-office; multi-currency wallet, cross-border transfers, FX, merchant/agent flows; live on Play Store & App Store. Keep it about architecture and decisions, no figures.

title: Debugging Android WebViews with chrome://inspect
brief: Enable WebView.setWebContentsDebuggingEnabled; open chrome://inspect on desktop; inspect DOM, console, and network of an in-app WebView; why this is essential when embedding web content (e.g. charting libraries) in native apps.

title: When to reach for a message queue
brief: Async decoupling, smoothing spikes, retries and backpressure; RabbitMQ vs Kafka mental model; when a queue adds needless complexity; idempotent consumers and dead-letter queues.

title: Fine-tuning vs RAG vs prompting: picking the right one
brief: What each technique changes; cost and maintenance tradeoffs; RAG for fresh/private knowledge, fine-tuning for style/format/behavior, prompting first; a simple decision flow.

title: Shoonya ticketing: monolith to event-driven microservice
brief: Re-architected a broker's in-app ticketing into a standalone event-driven microservice (Next.js, NestJS, RabbitMQ, Redis); halved the user flow from 4 steps to 2; automated KYC/bank-verification/payments; audit-log layer. Focus on the design, not internal numbers.

title: Docker Compose for a clean local dev environment
brief: Define services, depends_on + healthchecks, named volumes for data, env files, and override files for local tweaks; one command to spin up API + worker + Redis; common gotchas.

title: Pagination done right: cursor vs offset
brief: Why offset pagination breaks on large/changing datasets; cursor (keyset) pagination with a stable sort key; encoding cursors; tradeoffs and when offset is fine.

title: Getting reliable JSON out of local models
brief: response_format json_schema vs json_object vs text; why strict schemas sometimes return empty on certain models; a robust pattern: try structured output, fall back to text + tolerant JSON extraction; lowering temperature for determinism.

title: Cryr: a credit-reporting platform on microservices + Plaid
brief: Built credit-handling business logic on a reusable service platform; Kafka/Redis/MySQL microservice backend; Plaid integration for bank-data access; a Flutter customer app. Architecture and integration focus.

title: Moving fast in a big codebase with ripgrep and fzf
brief: rg for instant code search with globs and types; fzf for fuzzy file/branch/history selection; wiring them together in the shell and editor; a few aliases that compound over time.

title: Structuring a NestJS project for scale
brief: Feature modules, providers and DI, separating domain/use-case/infra layers, DTO validation, and keeping controllers thin; where to put cross-cutting concerns; testing seams.

title: launchd vs cron for scheduled scripts on macOS
brief: Why launchd is the macOS-native scheduler; a LaunchAgent plist with StartCalendarInterval, RunAtLoad, KeepAlive; loading with launchctl; PATH gotchas (use a login shell); when a simple cron line still suffices.

title: IKF: an AI stock-picks module for Android
brief: A self-contained Android feature module (Kotlin/Compose) on MVVM + Hilt; integrated an external AI-forecast API; AI picks, holdings, performance, heat-map; full subscription lifecycle via in-app WebView checkout; Paging 3 + Room. Focus on module design.

title: Bruno: an offline, git-friendly API client
brief: Why move off cloud-synced API clients; Bruno stores collections as plain files you commit to the repo; environments, scripting, running in CI; team workflow benefits.

title: Redis caching patterns that actually help
brief: Cache-aside vs write-through; sensible TTLs; the thundering-herd problem and locking/jitter; cache invalidation strategy; what NOT to cache.

title: LoRA fine-tuning on a Mac: a practical primer
brief: What LoRA adapters are and why they're cheap to train; dataset shape; running a small fine-tune locally on Apple Silicon; evaluating the result; when a LoRA beats prompting.

title: Sapidblue Accelerator: plug-and-play micro-frontend services
brief: A library of standalone services (auth, comms, user/config, wallet, payments) on Angular + NestJS, composable into whole products via Module Federation; reused across multiple client builds. Focus on the composability design.

title: Everyday Git that pays off: aliases, worktrees, bisect
brief: A handful of aliases; git worktree for parallel branches without stashing; interactive rebase for clean history; git bisect to find the breaking commit fast.

title: Micro-frontends with Module Federation: the tradeoffs
brief: What problem MF actually solves (independent deploys, team autonomy); the costs (shared deps, version drift, contracts); when a monolith or a monorepo is the better call.

title: Automating your daily standup from your own activity
brief: Capture what you actually did (commits, screen activity), summarize with a local model into a short standup; scheduling it; privacy: keep it local and scope to dev work.

title: Real-time customer support with WebRTC and Janus
brief: Built social and real-time features for an omnichannel support platform (CXInfinity): Facebook/Twitter channels, plus WebRTC audio/video on the Janus media server, with chat/SMS/co-browse over WebSockets. Focus on the real-time architecture.

title: Audit logging you won't regret later
brief: What to log (who/what/when/before-after), making it append-only and tamper-evident, separating audit from app data, and querying it for compliance and debugging without slowing the hot path.

title: Taming a monorepo with npm/pnpm workspaces
brief: When a monorepo helps; workspaces layout; sharing internal packages; running scripts across packages; pnpm's disk/speed wins; pitfalls with hoisting and versioning.
